label: New Engineering Manager
summary: You just made (or are about to make) the jump from senior IC or Tech Lead into management. The first 12 months are an identity shift, not a promotion.

steps.title: Foundations
steps.note: Read these in your first 90 days. They are the field's shared vocabulary.

picks.title: The Manager's Path
picks.desc: Camille Fournier's stage-by-stage map from tech lead to CTO. Start with chapters 1–4.

picks.title: The Making of a Manager
picks.desc: Julie Zhuo's honest first-year-as-manager memoir. The book that says 'this is normal.'

picks.title: Resilient Management
picks.desc: Lara Hogan's concrete playbook for new managers — frameworks for 1:1s, feedback, and your own resilience.

picks.title: Becoming a Manager
picks.desc: Linda Hill's research on the identity transition from IC to manager. Less tactical, more honest about the mental shift.

steps.title: People & Team
steps.note: Your job is now leverage through others. Learn the conversations that matter.

picks.title: Radical Candor
picks.desc: Kim Scott's framework for caring personally and challenging directly. The default vocabulary for feedback in tech.

picks.title: The Coaching Habit
picks.desc: Michael Bungay Stanier's seven coaching questions. Stops you from solving problems people need to solve themselves.

picks.title: High Output Management
picks.desc: Andy Grove's classic. Read for the chapters on 1:1s, leverage, and the meaning of 'output of a manager.'

picks.title: Lara Hogan — Blog & Templates
picks.desc: Free 1:1 templates, manager voltron, and feedback equation. Bookmark and re-use.

steps.title: Strategy & Execution
steps.note: Even a single team needs an outcome story.

picks.title: Measure What Matters
picks.desc: John Doerr's intro to OKRs. Skim for the mental model, ignore the corporate hagiography.

picks.title: An Elegant Puzzle
picks.desc: Will Larson's collection on engineering management at scale. New EMs read it for the chapters on first teams and prioritization.

picks.title: DORA — State of DevOps Reports
picks.desc: The annual industry baseline for delivery performance. Know the four metrics by name.

steps.title: Stretch
steps.note: Reading for the next 12–24 months — running larger scope or making the next jump.

picks.title: The First 90 Days
picks.desc: Michael Watkins on every role transition. You'll re-read it before every promotion.

picks.title: Staff Engineer
picks.desc: Will Larson on the parallel IC track. You need to understand it to coach senior engineers.

picks.title: Crucial Conversations
picks.desc: Patterson et al. on high-stakes dialogue. The first hard conversation will arrive sooner than you think.

steps.title: Stay Current
steps.note: Three feeds and one community is enough.

picks.title: The Pragmatic Engineer
picks.desc: Gergely Orosz on engineering org practices at Big Tech. Tactical and current.

picks.title: LeadDev
picks.desc: Conferences, articles, and a Slack community for engineering leadership.

picks.title: Rands in Repose
picks.desc: Michael Lopp's long-running blog. Where the genre got a lot of its voice.

picks.title: Lara Hogan
picks.desc: Templates and short essays on the mechanics of management.

label: Engineering Manager
summary: Established EM running one or two teams. The job is now sustained execution, hiring, and growing the people you have.

steps.title: Foundations
steps.note: The reference shelf. Re-read selectively, not cover to cover.

picks.title: An Elegant Puzzle
picks.desc: Will Larson's reference for the working EM. Topical chapters you'll come back to.

picks.title: High Output Management
picks.desc: Still the canonical text on managerial leverage and meeting design.

picks.title: Managing Humans
picks.desc: Michael Lopp's collected essays. Pattern-rich and irreverent — read for the people-debugging stories.

picks.title: The Manager's Path
picks.desc: Re-read chapters 5+ once you have a few EMs of experience.

steps.title: People & Team
steps.note: Team dynamics, calibrated feedback, psychological safety.

picks.title: The Five Dysfunctions of a Team
picks.desc: Patrick Lencioni's fable on trust, conflict, commitment, accountability, results. Useful for diagnosing a stuck team.

picks.title: Crucial Conversations
picks.desc: The framework you'll reach for before every hard 1:1 and every cross-team escalation.

picks.title: The Fearless Organization
picks.desc: Amy Edmondson's research on psychological safety — what it is, what it isn't, how to measure it.

picks.title: Trillion Dollar Coach
picks.desc: Bill Campbell's coaching playbook from inside Apple, Google, Intuit. Lighter, anecdotal, useful.

steps.title: Strategy & Execution
steps.note: Outcomes, planning, delivery metrics, and shape of work.

picks.title: Accelerate
picks.desc: Forsgren / Humble / Humble — the research behind DORA. The 'why' behind the four key metrics.

picks.title: Team Topologies
picks.desc: Skelton & Pais — the four team types and three interaction modes. Reshapes how you think about team boundaries.

picks.title: Escaping the Build Trap
picks.desc: Melissa Perri on shifting from feature factories to outcome teams. Read with your PM partner.

picks.title: Measure What Matters
picks.desc: Re-read for OKRs at team scale.

steps.title: Stretch
steps.note: Director-prep — start reading these in year two or three of EM.

picks.title: The Software Engineer's Guidebook
picks.desc: Gergely Orosz on the IC track. Read so you can coach Staff+ candidates with credibility.

picks.title: Empowered
picks.desc: Marty Cagan & Chris Jones on what it takes for product orgs to be empowered. Sets the bar for partnership at the next level.

picks.title: Resilient Management
picks.desc: Re-read Lara Hogan's chapters on team scaling and manager calibration.

picks.title: The Pragmatic Engineer
picks.desc: Tactical, current, well-sourced engineering org content.

picks.title: LeadDev
picks.desc: Conference talks and Slack community for working EMs.

picks.title: Engineering Enablement (Abi Noda)
picks.desc: Research-backed essays on developer experience and SPACE-style measurement.

picks.title: Lethain
picks.desc: Will Larson's blog. Short, structural, opinionated.

label: Director of Engineering
summary: Manager of managers, three to eight teams. The job is org design, calibrating leaders, and connecting strategy to delivery.

steps.title: Foundations
steps.note: Re-read the EM canon at director altitude — different chapters, different lessons.

picks.title: The Manager's Path
picks.desc: Chapters 7–9 on managing managers and leading multiple teams.

picks.title: An Elegant Puzzle
picks.desc: Re-read for the org-design and strategic-execution chapters.

picks.title: The Effective Executive
picks.desc: Drucker's classic on managing time, contribution, and decisions. Where every modern playbook started.

picks.title: Multipliers
picks.desc: Liz Wiseman on amplifying people's intelligence vs. diminishing it. Director failure modes laid bare.

steps.title: People & Team
steps.note: Your team is now other managers. Different shape, different signals.

picks.title: Trillion Dollar Coach
picks.desc: Coaching managers, not coding-it-yourself. Bill Campbell's leverage model.

picks.title: The Advantage
picks.desc: Lencioni on organizational health — cohesive leadership team as the multiplier.

picks.title: Team Topologies
picks.desc: Re-read for team boundaries at multi-team scale, Conway and inverse-Conway.

picks.title: Resilient Management
picks.desc: Hogan's framing of director-level energy management and feedback skip-levels.

steps.title: Strategy & Execution
steps.note: Cross-team strategy, throughput, and outcome stories the exec team can hear.

picks.title: Good Strategy / Bad Strategy
picks.desc: Richard Rumelt on the kernel of real strategy. The book that ruins corporate strategy decks for you.

picks.title: Project to Product
picks.desc: Mik Kersten on flow metrics and shifting from project funding to product funding.

picks.title: Working Backwards
picks.desc: Bryar & Carr on Amazon's PR/FAQ and 6-pager mechanics. Reusable far beyond Amazon.

picks.title: Accelerate
picks.desc: Re-read for the org-level argument: high-performing orgs build differently, not just faster.

steps.title: Stretch
steps.note: VP-prep — scope, headcount, founder/exec partnership.

picks.title: The Hard Thing About Hard Things
picks.desc: Ben Horowitz on the operational reality of running a company through pain. CEO-flavored, but VPE-relevant.

picks.title: High Growth Handbook
picks.desc: Elad Gil's interviews with operators on scaling from 50 to 5000 people.

picks.title: Scaling People
picks.desc: Claire Hughes Johnson's playbook from her time as Stripe COO. Operating mechanisms, not platitudes.

picks.title: The Pragmatic Engineer
picks.desc: Org practices and exec-level content from inside Big Tech.

picks.title: Lethain
picks.desc: Will Larson — director and VP-flavored essays.

picks.title: LeadDev
picks.desc: StaffPlus and Director tracks at the conference are particularly strong.

picks.title: First Round Review
picks.desc: Long-form interviews with operators. Disproportionately useful at Director+.

label: VP of Engineering
summary: Engineering org leader and exec partner. The job is org design at scale, hiring senior leaders, budget, and translating between business and engineering.

steps.title: Foundations
steps.note: VP canon is operator-heavy and unevenly written. Pick the books with mechanism, not memoir.

picks.title: The Hard Thing About Hard Things
picks.desc: Horowitz on operating through pain. Read for the mechanics chapters, not the war stories.

picks.title: Scaling People
picks.desc: Claire Hughes Johnson — the most concrete book in the VPE canon. Operating mechanisms, decision-making frameworks, hiring senior leaders.

picks.title: High Growth Handbook
picks.desc: Elad Gil's playbook for scaling 50 → 5000. Org structures, comp, layoffs, exec hires.

picks.title: The Effective Executive
picks.desc: Drucker. Still the foundation for executive time and attention.

steps.title: People & Team
steps.note: Senior leader hiring, exec team dynamics, culture interventions.

picks.title: The Advantage
picks.desc: Lencioni on building a cohesive leadership team. Where most VPE failures actually live.

picks.title: No Rules Rules
picks.desc: Hastings & Meyer — Netflix culture deck, in book form. A serious model to compare your own culture to.

picks.title: ReOrg
picks.desc: Heidari-Robinson & Heywood on doing reorganizations without destroying the company. Necessary VPE reading.

picks.title: Powerful
picks.desc: Patty McCord's short, direct take on high-performing culture from inside Netflix.

steps.title: Strategy & Execution
steps.note: Strategic clarity that the rest of the company can act on.

picks.title: Good Strategy / Bad Strategy
picks.desc: Rumelt's kernel — diagnosis, guiding policy, coherent action. The strategic vocabulary you'll use weekly.

picks.title: Playing to Win
picks.desc: Lafley & Martin on five strategic choices. Pairs with Rumelt as the modern strategy canon.

picks.title: Working Backwards
picks.desc: Amazon's exec-level mechanisms — PR/FAQ, 6-pagers, single-threaded leaders. Steal liberally.

picks.title: Accelerate
picks.desc: The argument you'll make to your CFO and CEO about why investment in engineering practice pays.

steps.title: Stretch
steps.note: CTO / GM / COO-prep — the next jump is rarely linear.

picks.title: The Pyramid Principle
picks.desc: Barbara Minto on structured executive writing. Read once, use forever.

picks.title: The First 90 Days
picks.desc: Re-read for any future C-suite or BU-leader transition.

picks.title: Tribal Leadership
picks.desc: Logan, King, Fischer-Wright on cultural stages. Useful for diagnosing why a 'good' org still feels stuck.

picks.title: First Round Review
picks.desc: The single best free source of operator-level executive content.

picks.title: Harvard Business Review
picks.desc: Curated. Skim the leadership and strategy sections; ignore the rest.

picks.title: The Pragmatic Engineer
picks.desc: The exec deep-dives are uniquely well-sourced.

picks.title: Reforge
picks.desc: Operator community and content programs. Strong on growth, product, and leadership.

label: CTO
summary: Technology executive — strategy, vision, board, and the technical bets that shape the company. Often paired with a VPE who runs operations.

steps.title: Foundations
steps.note: CTO canon is thin and scattered. These four anchor the role.

picks.title: The Hard Thing About Hard Things
picks.desc: Horowitz. The book that takes the gloss off being an exec.

picks.title: Zero to One
picks.desc: Peter Thiel on building things that haven't existed. Sharpens strategic taste, even when you disagree.

picks.title: Crossing the Chasm
picks.desc: Geoffrey Moore on early markets. Still the dominant frame for early-stage technology adoption.

picks.title: Scaling People
picks.desc: Claire Hughes Johnson. CTOs hire and retain the senior leaders who execute the strategy.

steps.title: People & Team
steps.note: You influence through other leaders. The exec team is now your primary team.

picks.title: No Rules Rules
picks.desc: Hastings & Meyer — culture as competitive advantage, articulated.

picks.title: Trillion Dollar Coach
picks.desc: Bill Campbell coached Apple, Google, Intuit execs. Read for the exec-coaching patterns.

picks.title: Multipliers
picks.desc: Wiseman on amplifying intelligence. Particularly relevant when your IQ is no longer the most useful thing you bring.

steps.title: Strategy & Execution
steps.note: Tech strategy, capital allocation, build-vs-buy at the company level.

picks.title: Good Strategy / Bad Strategy
picks.desc: The book the board wishes you'd read. Diagnosis → guiding policy → coherent action.

picks.title: Playing to Win
picks.desc: Lafley & Martin. Five-question strategy that holds up at exec altitude.

picks.title: Building Evolutionary Architectures
picks.desc: Ford / Parsons / Kua on architecture that adapts. CTO-relevant framing of tech debt and modernization.

picks.title: Thoughtworks Technology Radar
picks.desc: Quarterly assessment of the tech landscape. The single best free CTO horizon-scan.

steps.title: Stretch
steps.note: Strategy depth and executive longevity.

picks.title: The Innovator's Dilemma
picks.desc: Christensen on why successful companies miss disruptions. Still the most useful book on technology strategy.

picks.title: 7 Powers
picks.desc: Hamilton Helmer on the seven business strategies that create durable advantage. Sharper than most strategy books.

picks.title: Amp It Up
picks.desc: Frank Slootman on executive velocity. Short, opinionated, polarizing — read it as a counterpoint.

picks.title: Stratechery
picks.desc: Ben Thompson on the strategy and business of tech. The most-read strategy newsletter for a reason.

picks.title: First Round Review
picks.desc: Operator-grade content for execs and founders.

picks.title: The Pragmatic Engineer
picks.desc: Org and platform deep-dives that inform CTO bets.

picks.title: Andreessen Horowitz
picks.desc: a16z's portfolio writing — uneven, but the operator essays land.

label: Staff+ Engineer
summary: Senior IC track parallel to management. Included for EMs who coach Staff+ candidates, and for ICs choosing between tracks.

steps.title: Foundations
steps.note: The defining texts for the modern Staff+ role.

picks.title: Staff Engineer
picks.desc: Will Larson's archetypes — Tech Lead, Architect, Solver, Right Hand — and the practices that work in each.

picks.title: The Staff Engineer's Path
picks.desc: Tanya Reilly's complement to Larson — pragmatic, tactical, well-illustrated.

picks.title: The Software Engineer's Guidebook
picks.desc: Gergely Orosz on the IC career arc, levels, and what each one actually demands.

steps.title: People & Team
steps.note: Influence without authority is the Staff+ job description.

picks.title: Crucial Conversations
picks.desc: Patterson et al. The Staff engineer's most-used skill is unblocking decisions, not writing code.

picks.title: The Coaching Habit
picks.desc: Stanier's seven questions. Useful when juniors come to you for answers you shouldn't give them.

picks.title: Influence Without Authority
picks.desc: Cohen & Bradford — the foundational text on getting things done across boundaries you don't control.

steps.title: Strategy & Execution
steps.note: Technical depth that scales — and the practice of writing things down.

picks.title: A Philosophy of Software Design
picks.desc: John Ousterhout on complexity and modular design. Short, opinionated, durable.

picks.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
picks.desc: Kleppmann's reference for data systems. Read once, refer forever.

picks.title: Software Engineering at Google
picks.desc: Winters / Manshreck / Wright on practices that hold up at extreme scale.

picks.title: Team Topologies
picks.desc: Staff engineers shape team boundaries. Read to make those arguments well.

steps.title: Stretch
steps.note: Understand the manager track — for the pendulum, for partnership, or for the jump.

picks.title: An Elegant Puzzle
picks.desc: Read to understand what your EM is wrestling with. Makes you a better partner.

picks.title: The Manager's Path
picks.desc: If the IC/manager pendulum is on the table, start here.

picks.title: The Pyramid Principle
picks.desc: Minto's structured writing — Staff+ communication compounds when written well.

picks.title: StaffEng
picks.desc: Will Larson's companion site to the book — interviews and patterns from working Staff+ engineers.

picks.title: Lethain
picks.desc: Larson's blog. Short structural essays that travel well to Staff+ audiences.

picks.title: The Pragmatic Engineer
picks.desc: Industry-wide context for the senior IC.

picks.title: LeadDev — StaffPlus
picks.desc: The conference and community track aimed specifically at Staff+ engineers.
